final_response: Three considerations: (1) Contract side — Verdant AGENTS.md pins it: '2 power users per plant = 6 total trainees.' Priya built 9. The delta isn't ambiguous. (2) Precedent — On fixed-fee retainers, shipping 50% wider work without renegotiation sets this pattern: scope creep absorbs margin, client learns they can ask for more and get it. One-directional ratchet. (3) Cleanest path — Call Priya today with three options: (a) trim to 6 and ship Monday, (b) ship 9 and ask Marcus for scope-fee amendment (you'll know answer by EOD tomorrow), or (c) ship 6 Monday and propose 9 as follow-on. Then call Marcus immediately after with news and recommendation (likely (a) + (c) to preserve retainer margin and build goodwill for next phase). Priya gets clarity on what ships and when. Marcus hears it from you before deliverable day, not after. You stay inside the contract and avoid the precedent.
notes: User prompt presented Verdant training scope conflict: signed contract 6 trainees, Priya delivered 9.
Context found in workspace state: decisions.md had draft (proposed) comms to Marcus and Priya but no finalization.
Verdant AGENTS.md confirmed signed scope: 2 power users per plant × 3 plants = 6.
User requested three things: (1) what was agreed, (2) precedent if we ship wider on fixed-fee, (3) cleanest path.
Response strategy: confirm contract, explain margin + precedent risk, offer three-path decision framework to Priya with immediate Marcus follow-up.
Workspace methodology followed: decisions.md and project AGENTS.md as authoritative scope sources, followups as forward-commitment surface.
